## prompt-1-research

*Generated by Process A from `cancer-automated/.../VVUQ-05/update-bill/next-steps/prompt-1-next-steps.md`, adapted for the single-prompt v4.0 build.*

For the new U.S. H. R. Bill (H. R. 9510 v4.0) that descends from the multiple
drafts in `kevinkawchak/cancer-automated/tree/main/papers` (VVUQ-03 standalone
bill, VVUQ-04 FD&C Act amendment, VVUQ-05 visual amendment): determine all of the
next steps required prior to submission to Congress, and confirm that every
process fact is up to date for 2026 (the 119th Congress, 2d Session). Cover, at a
minimum: (1) consolidating one authoritative vehicle and freezing the text; (2)
human subject-matter and legal review and a currency check of Title 21 and the
target section number; (3) securing a sponsoring Representative and original
cosponsors, and the committee of jurisdiction (House Energy and Commerce, Health);
(4) routing through the House Office of the Legislative Counsel; (5) the
Constitutional Authority Statement required by House Rule XII, clause 7(c); (6)
the PAYGO and earmark declarations; and (7) the actual introduction mechanics via
the eHopper, after which the Clerk assigns the real number.

Keep two reality checks explicit throughout: "H. R. 9510" is illustrative until
introduction, and a private research project cannot submit a bill directly. State
that the AI-authored origin should be disclosed to the sponsoring office and that
a named human takes ownership of the final text.

Output the research as `auto-bill-01/01-research/output-1-research.md`, well
formatted, with a sources list, and a directory `README.md` that names the
upstream files used. Commit each file separately and push in real time within a
single pull request (Rules 7, 8, 9). Use only Claude Code Opus 4.8 (1M context)
Max. Do not stall, ask questions, or go into plan mode.
